description: YOMI-style matchup relationships for player understanding. These are explanation/education overlays derived from the canonical action labels and physical profiles, not a separate truth system.

matchup_rules.explanation: Guard absorbs the edge and preserves balance; attacker loses tempo.
Guard deflects the thrust line; attacker must reset.
Grab telegraphs against a set guard and may be punished.
Shove breaks guard stance and forces a step back.
Kick punishes the static guard by striking below the weapon.
Cut catches a fighter stepping into measure.
Recovery is vulnerable to a committed cut.
Brace absorbs the cut through armor rather than guarding it.
Thrust punishes a fighter stepping into point range.
Pivot rotates off the thrust line and evades.
Braced stance absorbs the bash impact.
Braced stance resists the shove displacement.
A committed cut can overwhelm a brace if the bracing fighter is too slow.
Bash crashes through the guard and staggers the defender.
Bash punishes a recovering fighter who cannot brace in time.
Hook bind traps the guarding weapon and denies its line.
Grab reaches past the weapon guard to control the limb.
Shove displaces the guarding fighter and opens their line.
Kick and brace trade: the kicker gains space but the bracer holds ground.
A well-timed step can evade a thrust, but mistiming invites the point.
